name: Tofu/Miso/Umeboshi (Salted Preserved Plums) Okayu
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Short-grained Japanese style rice (wash thoroughly)
2 3 rounded tablespoons white miso paste (more or less to taste)
1-2 large salted preserved plums (umeboshi, take seeds out and cut into small pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. "Okayu" - Japanese-style porridge.
2. While waiting for water to boil:
a. Break apart tofu into small chunks (dice or use hands)
b. Wash and cut spinach into 1/4-inch segments
c. Wash and cut nappa cabbage into 1/4-inch segments
d. Remove seeds from umeboshi and cut into small pieces
3. When water is at a nice rolling boil:
a. Add miso paste, ensuring it dissolves completely in the water
b. Taste and adjust by adding more miso or water to achieve desired flavor
c. Add rice, tofu, spinach, nappa cabbage, and umeboshi
d. If using, add mirin or sake
4. Once the mixture returns to a rolling boil, cover the pot and reduce heat to low simmer for 30 minutes. Stir occasionally.
5. Cook further if desired for a creamier porridge consistency.
6. This makes a nice replacement for soup on a cold day. For added flavor as a garnish, puree some raw daikon with a tiny amount of fresh ginger, lemon juice, and soy sauce.
